title: brucea antidysenterica
intro: Bracea antidysenterica, also known as the Qilin or Qilin tree, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, recognized for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, in particular, have been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including dysentery, fever, and inflammation, due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Brucea antidysenterica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infection, making it a valuable remedy for treating various health conditions. The plant can be prepared as an herbal medicine in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tablets, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
